name: Winston Lin
title: Products, workflows, and systems in practice
tagline: I work across cloud products, developer platforms, AI workflows, and supporting tools. I’m usually most useful where product judgment, technical detail, and operating reality all matter at once: platform decisions, workflow design, operating clarity, and the seams between teams. I care about work that teams can understand, trust, and rely on day to day. I also advise and invest in small businesses.
meta_description: Case studies, writing, and small tools from cloud products, developer platforms, AI workflows, and business systems.

oracle_blog_links.title: OCI Functions in Customer Workflows: Common Patterns and Practical Design Checklists
A Practical Guide to Cross-Region Recovery for OCI Functions
Introducing Long-Running Functions and Response Destinations with OCI Functions

intro_paragraphs: I’ve worked across cloud products, developer platforms, AI workflows, and business systems, and I also work with operators and founders on growth and service design.
experience_highlights: Principal Product Manager for OCI Functions, focused on developer experience and execution model
Previously led product work across OCI DevOps / CI/CD and adjacent platform systems
Built internal platform capabilities that shortened delivery cycles and reduced operational drag

intro: I'm based in San Francisco and work across cloud products, developer tools, and AI workflows. I'm usually most useful where product judgment, technical detail, and operating reality all matter at once, especially around workflows, documentation, and the seams between teams.

Lately I've been using AI in a more hands-on way, with real workspaces, files, scripts, docs, and review loops instead of one-off prompting. That has made me think even more about tools, documentation, and handoffs that hold up in real work.
I'm comfortable working across engineers, operators, leadership, and business stakeholders. Outside of work, I also advise and invest in small businesses and startups in the U.S. and Asia.
background: I grew up between Taipei and Orange County, which gave me a mix of perspectives early on and made me comfortable moving between different environments.

I later earned a B.S. from UC Berkeley Haas, where I got more interested in the overlap between business and technology. I also spent a lot of time in tutoring, mentorship, and lab assistant roles, and stayed involved after college as a mentor in the undergraduate program through 2025.
In school, I was deeply into competitive math and community volunteering. That mix of analytical work and helping other people still shapes how I think about problems and how I like to work.
personal: Outside of work, I try to stay active and get outdoors when I can, whether that means hiking around the Bay Area, exploring new places, or just getting out of the city for a bit. I also shoot photography, mostly on Fujifilm, as a way to slow down and capture things that feel real in the moment.

I grew up playing sports and still play basketball and volleyball when I can. More recently I’ve also gotten into running and Barry's classes. I also like anime (Big Fan of Frieren and Your Lie in April). Lately I’ve been spending more time thinking about investing, real estate, and how people make long-term bets on assets and businesses.
